seoTitle: Move a Page to Another Workspace in Microsoft Loop
metaDescription: Relocate a Microsoft Loop page, along with its nested subpages, to a completely different workspace without manually copying content.
intro: If a page ends up filed under the wrong project, you don't need to manually rebuild it in the right workspace. This guide shows how to move a page — including any nested subpages — directly from one Microsoft Loop workspace to another using the sidebar's built-in transfer tool.

steps.heading: Open the source workspace
steps.body: Go to loop.cloud.microsoft and navigate to the workspace containing the page you want to move.

steps.heading: Find the page in the sidebar
steps.body: Go to the left sidebar content tree and locate your target page or subpage.

steps.heading: Open the more options menu
steps.body: Hover over the title, click the three dots icon, and open the more options menu.

steps.heading: Select the move command
steps.body: Choose 'Move to Another Workspace' from the dropdown to trigger the destination selection drawer.

steps.heading: Pick the destination workspace
steps.body: Click the destination workspace name from your populated account directory list.

steps.heading: Execute the move and verify
steps.body: Click the Move button to transfer the page, then navigate to the new workspace to confirm the page and its content loaded successfully.

faq.question: Will moving a page also move its subpages?
faq.answer: Yes, moving a parent page automatically pulls all of its nested child subpages along with it.

faq.question: Will collaborators lose access after a page is moved?
faq.answer: It's worth reviewing permission levels after a move, since existing collaborators' access can be affected by the change in workspace.

faq.question: Does moving a page duplicate its content?
faq.answer: No, the transfer relocates the page rather than creating a duplicate, so you won't end up with copies in both workspaces.

faq.question: How do I confirm a move worked correctly?
faq.answer: Navigate to the destination workspace and refresh the sidebar to confirm the page and its tables loaded successfully.
